title: Garlic & Parmesan Roast Potatoes
summary: Crispy, crunchy, and loaded with parmesan. This recipe for roast potatoes is a winner. You'll never want to go back to regular roast potatoes ever again.

method.description: Peel your potatoes and place them in a large saucepan, cover with cold water and season generously.
Put the potatoes on a high heat to par-boil for 10-12 mins. Once the potatoes are partially cooked, drain, give them a shake to ruffle the outsides and leave them to steam dry for 5 mins.
In the meantime, put your oil and butter in a saucepan and preheat on the stove. Preheat a tray in the oven.
Remove the tray from the oven and carefully add the potatoes and the hot oil, season with salt. Using a couple of forks, turn the potatoes in the hot fat and get them back in the oven.
Cook for 25 mins then remove from the oven.
Give the potatoes a shake and turn any that are colouring quickly, return the tray to the oven and continue cooking until the potatoes are nice and roasty.
Grate some garlic into 5 tbsp olive oil and toss through the potatoes with the rosemary. Cover with half the parmesan and return to the oven for the last 3-4 mins.
Serve with extra flaky salt, crispy rosemary and grated parm on top.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
